subject: Chemistry
title: Germanium Proves Mendeleev Right
description: Learn Germanium Proves Mendeleev Right in one minute.

hook.text: In 1886, germanium was discovered in a silver mine. Its properties matched Mendeleev's 1871 predictions almost perfectly.

buildup.text: Mendeleev had called it eka-silicon and predicted its atomic weight, density, and oxide formula.

discovery.text: Clemens Winkler isolated it and found: weight 72.6 (predicted 72), density 5.47 (predicted 5.5).

twist.text: Winkler wanted to name it neptunium, but that name was already taken. He chose germanium for Germany.

climax.text: Germanium later became crucial for transistors—the building blocks of every computer.

punchline.text: Predicted in 1871, found in 1886. Now inside your electronics.

quiz.question: How close were Mendeleev's predictions for germanium?
options: Almost exact—weight and density matched within 1%
He got the weight right but density wrong
His predictions were only vaguely close
